<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塚廻古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塚廻古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塚廻古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Tsukamawari Kofun.JPG|location={{ill|Sakai-ku|WD=Q1042008|1=Sakai-ku, Sakai}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.56277778|N|135.49066667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}}|part_of={{ill|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|1=Mozu Tombs}}}}
{{ill|User:Immanuelle|ja|User:Immanuelle|lt=Immanuelle|lt_ja=Immanuelle|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/User:Immanuelle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|User talk:Immanuelle|ja|User talk:Immanuelle|lt=talk|lt_ja=talk|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/User talk:Immanuelle|qq=}}) 08:01, 16 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{ill|Tsukamawari Kofun|ja|塚廻古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamawari Kofun|qq=}} {{Other uses||Kofun in Kōnan, Osaka Prefecture|Tsukamari Kofun}} {{Do not confuse with|{{ill|Tsukamawari Kofun Group|ja|塚廻り古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamawari Kofun Group|qq=}}}}
{{Japanese Kofun |name = Tsukamawari Kofun |image = [[File:Tsukamawari Kofun.JPG|250px]] | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Kofun Cluster|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai City|1=Sakai}} {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|en|Sakai-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042008|lt=Sakai Ward|1=Sakai-ku, Sakai}} Mozu Yuun Town |coordinates = {{coord|34|33|46.0|N|135|29|26.4|E|region:JP-27}} |type = Circular Kofun |map_size = Diameter 35 m |built = 5th century |burial = Wooden coffin |Interred persons = |excavations = Bronze mirror, jade magatama, iron sword |Historic Site Historic Site = National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}} |notes = }}
'''Tsukamawari Kofun''' (塚廻古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=kofun|1=Kofun}} ({{ill|Circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) located in Mozu Yuun Town, {{ill|Sakai-ku, Sakai|en|Sakai-ku, Sakai|WD=Q1042008|lt=Sakai Ward|1=Sakai-ku, Sakai}} of {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|WD=Q193428|lt=Sakai City|1=Sakai}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Kofun Cluster|1=Mozu Tombs}}, and is considered to be an {{ill|accompanying mound|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/accompanying mound|1=Baizuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658220}} of the nearby {{ill|Daisen burial Mound|ja|大仙陵古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen burial Mound|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Daisenryō Kofun|WD=Q5849431}}. It has been Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}.
== Overview == The mound is located on the eastern side of the {{ill|Daisen burial Mound|ja|大仙陵古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisen burial Mound|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Daisenryō Kofun|WD=Q5849431}}, adjacent to its surrounding moat, and is considered to be one of its accompanying mounds. The tumulus is a circular mound with a diameter of just over 35 m and a height of 4 to 5 m, with a row of haniwa figures observed along its base. Recent investigations have confirmed the existence of a surrounding moat. The internal structure consists of a wooden coffin. In 1912 (Meiji 45), an excavation survey was conducted by {{ill|Tsuboi Shogo|ja|坪井正五郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuboi Shogo|1=Tsuboi Shōgorō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425705}}, during which, from a centrally sunken wooden board measuring 3 m in length and 54 cm in width, a detypeed four-beast mirror, a detypeed five-beast mirror, an iron sword, 2–3 iron daggers, nephrite, jade, glass, 7 talc-made magatama, 4 jade date beads, 70 jade tubular beads, over 1,000 small glass beads, and several hundred talc-made mortar beads were excavated (the burial goods are preserved by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|en|Archives and Mausolea Department|WD=Q4346533|lt=Imperial Household Agency burial Office|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}). Among the accompanying mounds of the Daisen burial Mound, it is significant as a tumulus with clearly defined internal structure and associated grave goods. In 1958 (Showa 33), it was Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|WD=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}.
== Nature of the Excavated Wooden Board and the 1912 Excavation Proceedings == {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koichi Mori|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}} suggested that the centrally sunken wooden board, measuring 3 meters in length as mentioned above, is likely the bottom of a {{ill|Bamboo-split Wooden Coffin|ja|割竹形木棺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bamboo-split Wooden Coffin|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398019}}, although he also noted the possibility that a log boat was used as a coffin, and he could not conclusively determine which was the case. The 1912 excavation was carried out at the initiative of the landowner and was not strictly a scholarly investigation; in truth, {{ill|Tsuboi Shogo|ja|坪井正五郎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuboi Shogo|1=Tsuboi Shōgorō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425705}} was merely summoned to attend and provide his opinion, and the excavation report was nothing more than an article contributed by a newspaper reporter covering the excavation for an "Archaeology Magazine". Furthermore, the excavation was hastily concluded in just two days, and immediately afterward, a telegram was sent from the {{ill|Imperial Household Ministry burial Office|ja|宮内省諸陵寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Ministry burial Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (currently {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|en|Archives and Mausolea Department|WD=Q4346533|lt=Imperial Household Agency burial Office|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}) ordering its suspension, following intypeation that an unregistered accompanying mound had been excavated. Additionally, {{ill|Katsumi Kuroban|ja|黒板勝美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsumi Kuroban|1=Katsumi Kuroita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3894478}}—a lecturer in national history at Tokyo Imperial University, like Tsuboi—harshly criticized this excavation in the same issue of the Archaeology Magazine that published the report on the Tsukamawari Kofun, and he submitted a paper arguing that legislation should be enacted to restrict kofun excavations. Moreover, a lawsuit continued for some time between the government and the landowner over the ownership of the excavated burial goods <ref>Mori (1965) pp.47-51</ref>.
== References == * Takafumi Matsumura, "Tsukamawari Kofun", Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun, Tokyo-do Publishing, 1989, p. 374 * {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koichi Mori|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}}, "Unregistered Accompanying Mound", Excavation of Kofun, Chuko Shinsho 65, Chuo Koronsha, 1965, pp. 47–51
== Footnotes ==
{{Reflist|2}}
== External Links == [http://bunka8.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=139017 Tsukamawari Kofun] (Historic Site Online)
